summary: All three fixes from Round 1 landed correctly and introduce no regressions. The four deferred findings (Critical #1, Major #2, Minor #4, Minor #5) are re-assessed as acceptable given their stated rationale. One out-of-scope fix (protobuf CVE-2024-7254 bump) is noted as correct. No new findings.

fix_verification.title: Explicit parentFile?.mkdirs() in DataStore produceFile
fix_verification.detail: CoreSettingsModule.kt line 40: `File(context.filesDir, DATASTORE_FILE).also { it.parentFile?.mkdirs() }`. The lambda is correctly scoped inside `produceFile = { ... }`. The safe-call `?.mkdirs()` handles the edge case where `parentFile` is null (impossible for a two-segment path but defensive). No regression risk: `also` returns the File receiver, so DataStoreFactory.create receives the correct File argument unchanged.

fix_verification.title: Smoke path corrected in task.md
fix_verification.detail: work/phase-2-ui/tasks/2.md: path updated to `build/generated/sources/proto/debug/java/...` (sources plural, debug variant). Inline parenthetical note explains the reason (protobuf-gradle-plugin 0.9.5 Android variant output). No regression.

fix_verification.title: Protobuf bumped 4.26.1 → 4.28.3 (CVE-2024-7254)
fix_verification.detail: Not a Round 1 finding but included in the same commit. The version bump in gradle/libs.versions.toml is correct: 4.28.3 is the earliest 4.x release that patches CVE-2024-7254 (stack overflow in protobuf-javalite parsing of deeply nested messages). Both `protobuf-javalite` and `protoc` share the same `protobuf` version ref, so both are bumped consistently. No API-breaking changes between 4.26.x and 4.28.x for Java/Kotlin lite. No regression.

deferred_reassessment.title: Hilt plugin alias hilt.application on library module
deferred_reassessment.rationale: The Hilt artifact `com.google.dagger.hilt.android` is the single published Gradle plugin that handles both app and library modules via internal logic — no separate `hilt.android.library` plugin exists in the Dagger distribution. The patterns.md `Hilt module organization` section does not distinguish plugin aliases by module type. `:core-runtime` uses the identical alias and compiles successfully. The Round 1 recommendation to add `hilt-library` to the version catalog was based on Hilt documentation for older plugin versions (pre-2.41) where a separate plugin existed; current Hilt 2.x uses a single unified plugin. Closing as false positive — no action required.

deferred_reassessment.title: ErrorLog component 'settings-io' not in patterns.md whitelist
deferred_reassessment.rationale: Tech-spec decision D27 explicitly assigns whitelist enforcement to Task 3. The patterns.md whitelist is a code-review convention (no runtime enforcement today). Task 3 is documented as responsible for extending the closed set with 'settings-io', 'camera', 'audio', 'attachment-decode'. The cross-task dependency is documented. Acceptable for task 2 completion; must be verified closed by the Task 3 code reviewer.

deferred_reassessment.title: Test method named ioException_observeDoesNotCrash tests CorruptionException path
deferred_reassessment.rationale: task.md TDD anchor `ioException_observeDoesNotCrash` is the authoritative name per spec. Renaming would require updating the task spec and is out of scope for task 2. The test logic is correct: CorruptionException extends IOException so the `.catch(IOException)` handler covers it. A clarifying comment in the test would improve future maintainability but is not required for task 2 sign-off.

deferred_reassessment.title: Real ErrorLog in tests writes to Robolectric filesDir
deferred_reassessment.rationale: Making ErrorLog open or introducing an interface in :core-runtime is out of scope for task 2. Risk is low: JUnit 4 single-thread runner, Robolectric resets app state between tests, and no concurrent test execution is configured. Deferred to a future refactor task when ErrorLog interface extraction becomes necessary for other reasons.
